name: Mozambican Portuguese

ancestors.notes: Colonial and post-colonial European Portuguese standard. Mozambique was a Portuguese colony until independence in 1975; educated speech follows European norms more closely than Angola or Brazil.
Swahili substrate/adstrate: spoken across northern Mozambique (Cabo Delgado, Nampula) and as a trade language; some phonological patterns transferred.
Nyanja/Chewa substrate: major language of central Mozambique (Tete, Zambézia); five-vowel system transfers into Portuguese learner varieties.
Tsonga/Tswa-Ronga substrate: dominant in southern Mozambique (Gaza, Maputo provinces); influences tonality and vowel quality in Maputo Portuguese.

notes: Mozambican Portuguese (Português de Moçambique). Official language of Mozambique since independence (1975); spoken as L1 by ~12% and L2 by ~55% of the population (~32 million total). KEY FEATURES: (1) FIVE-VOWEL SYSTEM: unstressed vowel reduction is weaker than European Portuguese; /ɨ/ largely absent, unstressed /e/ → [e] not [ɨ]. (2) RHOTIC: typically alveolar trill [r] (as in Brazilian), not uvular [ʁ]; in coda position often weakened or deleted. (3) SIBILANTS: coda /s/ usually remains alveolar [s], not palatalised to [ʃ] (contrast European PT). (4) NASAL VOWELS preserved but with less nasalisation than EP. (5) TONE: influence of Bantu substrate languages creates pitch accent in some features. (6) /l/ in coda often vocalised to [w] (convergence with Brazilian). (7) Distinction /v/ vs /b/ maintained. Maputo (capital) speech is the most prestigious variety. Sources: Gonçalves (1996), Stroud & Gonçalves (1997), Lopes (2004).

sources.author: Gonçalves, P.
sources.title: Português de Moçambique: uma variedade em formação

sources.author: Stroud, C. & Gonçalves, P. (eds.)

sources.author: Lopes, A.J.
sources.title: A batalha das línguas: perspectivas sobre linguística aplicada em Moçambique

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
